description: Acrostic text validation for Hebrew Signs project — tests letter ordering against actual text

psalm_119.note: Each stanza begins with successive Hebrew letter. The psalmist deliberately chose this structure.

chapter_6q.6q_what: An extended acrostic meditation extolling the supreme value of Yahweh's law, precepts, statutes, commandments, and word as the source of blessing, purity, wisdom, revival, and eternal truth amid affliction, persecution, and temptation. The psalmist expresses wholehearted devotion, longing for divine teaching, and commitment to obedience while contrasting fidelity with the waywardness of the proud and wicked.
chapter_6q.6q_who: Psalmist (devout servant), Yahweh (divine lawgiver and reviver), the proud (cursed wanderers), princes (slanderers), the wicked (persecutors), those who fear Yahweh (fellow observers).
chapter_6q.6q_when: Undated post-exilic era, evoking timeless covenantal faithfulness across generations.
chapter_6q.6q_where: Earthly sojourn as a stranger, contrasted with heaven where Yahweh's word is eternally settled.
chapter_6q.6q_how: Acrostic psalm structured in 22 stanzas of eight verses each following the Hebrew alphabet, employing repetitive synonymous parallelism for Torah terms (law, statutes, precepts, commandments, word, testimonies, ordinances, judgments).
chapter_6q.6q_why: Affirms the eternality, perfection, and transformative power of God's word as life's guiding light, source of wisdom surpassing human insight, and anchor of hope in suffering, underscoring Torah piety as the path to blessing and ultimate salvation in redemptive history.
chapter_6q.6q_summary: The longest psalm celebrates unwavering devotion to Yahweh's multifaceted word as the ultimate delight, counselor, reviver, and light amid trials, surpassing all earthly treasures in value and efficacy.
